proposed_relations.description: Off-board fare collection is part of the Webster Avenue SBS (Bx41) project

evidence_refs.source_quote: 1. Before you board, dip your MetroCard at the MetroCard machine or insert coins into the coin machine located on the sidewalk 2. Take your proof of payment receipt 3. Enter through the front or rear door of bus - no need to show the receipt to the driver
▪ NYCT inspector teams conduct random checks of buses ▪ $100 fine for not having a receipt ▪ Fare evasion declined on the Bx12 and M15 after pre-payment was introduced

rationale: p012_c0003 describes the off-board fare payment process for the Bx41 SBS; p012_c0007 describes enforcement via NYCT inspectors. Both are from the Webster Avenue SBS presentation.

evidence_refs.source_quote: 1. Before you board, dip your MetroCard at the MetroCard machine or insert coins into the coin machine located on the sidewalk 2. Take your proof of payment receipt 3. Enter through the front or rear door of bus - no need to show the receipt to the driver
▪ NYCT inspector teams conduct random checks of buses ▪ $100 fine for not having a receipt ▪ Fare evasion declined on the Bx12 and M15 after pre-payment was introduced

quarantine.note: Cited blocks p012_c0003 and p012_c0007 describe the generic mechanics of off-board fare payment and enforcement but neither mentions Bx41, Webster Avenue, or the specific project. The connection to the Webster Avenue SBS project is inferred entirely from document co-occurrence, which the rules explicitly state is not a sufficient relation. (Note: p011_c0002 in the same source explicitly lists off-board fare under Bx41 SBS, but was not cited as evidence for this decision.)
